trades.stopLoss: Close if XLE < $62 or ceasefire announced
trades.thesis: Iran war Week 2: Strait of Hormuz closed, oil $115+ and heading to $130-150. Energy stocks (XLE) to follow with 50-70% of oil's move. Call spread limits premium risk in high-IV environment while capturing upside.
trades.catalyst: Continued Strait closure, Iran escalation, no ceasefire
trades.risk: Surprise ceasefire or regime capitulation could crater oil 20%+ rapidly
trades.notes: Wait for US market open (9:30 PM SGT) to assess gap. Adjust strikes up if XLE opens above $68.

trades.thesis: Direct Brent exposure for tactical oil trade. BNO tracks Brent (Middle East benchmark) better than USO for this crisis. Prefer buying dips rather than chasing at current levels.
trades.catalyst: Any pullback on ceasefire rumors = buying opportunity if fundamentals unchanged
trades.risk: Contango decay if held >4 weeks; sudden peace deal
trades.notes: Not chasing here. Wait for volatility-driven pullback.

trades.thesis: High oil = inflation = higher rates = bad for growth multiples. Tech/semis most vulnerable to multiple compression. Trim concentrated positions, don't panic sell.
trades.catalyst: Oil sustained above $100, inflation expectations rising
trades.notes: Don't sell into Monday's panic. Wait 2-3 days for dust to settle, then rebalance.
